scenario_title: Delete non-existent RRset as a semantic no-op or failure
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified choice point in RFC 2136 by updating an existing owner name whose target rrset is absent, then issuing a single delete_rrset for that missing type. The prerequisite rrset_absent makes the starting state explicit, and the follow-up QUERY confirms that both outcomes leave the zone unchanged. The only observable divergence is therefore the UPDATE RCODE, which is exactly the protocol gap being exercised.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset of an absent rrset as a benign no-op and accepts the UPDATE.

group_2.behavior: Treats delete_rrset of an absent rrset as a semantic error and rejects the UPDATE.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines delete_rrset syntax and prerequisite handling, but does not clearly mandate whether deleting an rrset that is already absent must succeed as an idempotent no-op or fail with NXRRSET (or another error). As a result, servers can diverge in the UPDATE reply while producing the same post-state.
